title: Introduction
description: NOC 00013 – Senior managers - health, education, social and community services and membership organizations | GTR Immigration Executives under NOC 00013 lead Canada’s essential "people-focused" sectors, including hospital networks, university systems, and major non-profit organizations. As a TEER 0 classification, this role is critical to Canada’s social fabric. For immigration, this NOC is a top priority in Healthcare and Education targeted draws, offering a prestigious and fast-track pathway for global leaders in social and health infrastructure.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category0 – Management occupationsTEER Level0Major Group00 – Legislative and senior management occupationsSub-Major Group000 – Legislative and senior management occupationsMinor Group0001 – Senior management occupationsNOC Code00013

duties: Establish objectives for the organization and develop or approve social, educational, or health policies and programs.
Direct the organization’s financial and human resources to ensure the delivery of essential services.
Represent the organization in negotiations with government bodies, labor unions, or other agencies.
Oversee the development of research, advocacy, and community outreach programs.
Evaluate the effectiveness of programs and implement strategic improvements.
employment_requirements: A university degree in a related field (e.g., Health Administration, Education, Social Work, Business) is required.
A master’s degree or PhD is frequently required for senior roles in education and health.
Extensive experience as a manager in the specific field is mandatory.
job_outlook: The outlook is Very Good. The expansion of healthcare facilities and the increasing importance of social advocacy organizations ensure a strong and growing market for senior leaders in these sectors.
labour_market_demand: Demand is Excellent, particularly in the healthcare and education sectors. Canada’s aging population and the expansion of the national childcare strategy have created an urgent need for executive directors and VPs to lead health authorities, social service agencies, and post-secondary institutions.
Priority Immigration Sector: Healthcare and Education leaders are currently the #1 priority for IRCC in 2026.
Mission-Driven Leadership: Lead organizations that are globally respected for their quality of care and research output.
Collaborative Ecosystem: Work closely with government and private partners to drive social innovation.
pathways: Express Entry Healthcare Draws: Direct priority for hospital and health authority executives.
Express Entry Education Draws: Targeted invitations for senior education administrators.
BC PNP Health Authority Stream: A dedicated path for those with offers from B.C. health regions.
